description: Basic PUBLISH message with application payload

autobahn-python:
from autobahn.wamp.message import Publish

# Test framework provides 'msg' with deserialized message
assert isinstance(msg, Publish)
assert msg.request == 239714735
assert msg.topic == 'com.myapp.mytopic1'
assert msg.args == ['Hello, world!']
assert msg.kwargs is None or msg.kwargs == {}

# Check that options are empty/default (all None)
assert msg.acknowledge is None
assert msg.exclude_me is None
assert msg.retain is None

construction.autobahn-python:
from autobahn.wamp.message import Publish

msg = Publish(
    request=239714735,
    topic='com.myapp.mytopic1',
    args=['Hello, world!']
)

autobahn-python:
from autobahn.wamp.message import Publish

# Test framework provides 'msg' with deserialized message
assert isinstance(msg, Publish)
assert msg.request == 123456789
assert msg.topic == 'com.myapp.encrypted'

# Transparent payload mode: args/kwargs are None, payload is bytes
assert msg.args is None
assert msg.kwargs is None
assert msg.payload is not None
assert isinstance(msg.payload, bytes)
assert msg.payload.hex() == '82a5636f6c6f72a66f72616e6765a573697a65739317002a07'

# E2E encryption metadata in options
assert msg.enc_algo == 'cryptobox'
assert msg.enc_serializer == 'msgpack'

construction.autobahn-python:
from autobahn.wamp.message import Publish

# Simulated encrypted payload (opaque bytes)
payload_bytes = bytes.fromhex('82a5636f6c6f72a66f72616e6765a573697a65739317002a07')

msg = Publish(
    request=123456789,
    topic='com.myapp.encrypted',
    payload=payload_bytes,
    enc_algo='cryptobox',
    enc_serializer='msgpack'
)

notes: Sample 1: Basic PUBLISH with normal payload mode (args/kwargs)
Sample 2: PUBLISH with transparent payload mode (E2E encryption)
Normal mode: Application payload in args/kwargs (router deserializes)
Transparent mode: Application payload as opaque bytes (router does NOT deserialize)
Transparent mode enables E2E encryption: router cannot read encrypted payload
enc_algo and enc_serializer in Options indicate encryption/serialization used
Payload bytes must be preserved exactly through router (byte-for-byte)
Serializers list contains valid byte representations (at least one must match)
Validation blocks are executable code (at least one must pass)
Construction creates a message instance for serialization tests
